description: Demo of GMPCs (US-130) — a full player character who fights at your side as a party member. Tamsin Reed, a Wizard, joins with her own spell slots, prepared spells, and action economy. She spawns beside you with a PC token, takes her own slot in the initiative order, and on her turn a deterministic combat AI resolves her actions instantly — she picks a target, moves into range, and casts from HER own slots (Magic Missile, then Fire Bolt once slots run dry), spending her resources, not yours. The goblins target and damage her like any combatant; the GM narrates her in voice. Click her token to inspect her. Verify: she rolls Initiative, her turn resolves immediately (no waiting), her spell VFX fire from HER token, her slots/HP update on her party entry, and the goblins come for her too.
customIntroduction: The old waystation is a roofless shell of fire-scorched stone, its yard littered with the wreck of the last caravan that sheltered here. You are not alone: Tamsin Reed keeps pace at your shoulder, sleeves pushed back, a cantrip already humming on her fingertips — she has thrown in with you, and she fights her own fights. Across the yard, goblins peel out from behind a toppled wagon, blades out, fanning to flank.
customContext:
DEMO — GMPCs (US-130). This is a COMBAT scene showcasing a GMPC — a full player character (a Wizard) fighting alongside the human player as a party member. Tamsin Reed (gmpc id 'gmpc_tamsin_reed') is listed in CURRENT STATE under PARTY → GMPCs with her kit (spell slots, prepared spells); she is referenced by her gmpc id, NOT an ally_X label.

HOW IT WORKS — IMPORTANT: Tamsin's MECHANICAL turn is resolved AUTOMATICALLY by the engine's deterministic combat AI. You do NOT roll her dice, move her, or call gmpc_act on her turn — the engine already moves her into range and casts from her own slots (Magic Missile first, then Fire Bolt), spends her action economy, and rolls the dice. The Event Log shows what she did.

YOUR JOB is to give her a VOICE: when she acts, drop a short in-character line with npc_speaks (entity 'gmpc_tamsin_reed') — terse, dry, focused — so she reads as a person, not a turret. React to the fight; don't restate the mechanics the log already shows, and don't re-resolve or override her turn.

1. Combat starts automatically (the goblins are hostile). Tamsin rolls Initiative and takes her own slot.
2. On her turn the engine resolves her action instantly; narrate it in her voice with npc_speaks.
3. The goblins target Tamsin as readily as the player; if she falls to 0 HP she's down and skipped until healed — you may play that for tension.
4. Leave the human player's own turn to them.

The point of the demo: a believable spellcaster ally whose mechanics are the real PC engine (leveled slots, prepared spells, action economy), resolved instantly, with your narration on top.
objective: Fight the goblins alongside Tamsin Reed — a wizard party member. Watch her turn resolve on its own: she moves up and casts from her own spell slots, the GM voices her, and the goblins target her too. Click her token to inspect her.

then.message: DEMO (US-130): Tamsin Reed fights beside you as a wizard party member. She rolls Initiative and takes her own turn — resolved instantly: she moves up and casts from HER own spell slots, while the GM gives her a voice. The goblins can target her too. Click her token to inspect her.
